# SkyPilot CLI Reference

Complete reference for all `sky` commands and options.

## Core Cluster Commands

### `sky launch`

Launch a cluster or task.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `ENTRYPOINT` ‚Äî text
- `--cluster`, `-c` ‚Äî A cluster name. If provided, either reuse an existing cluster with that name or provision a new cluster with that name. Otherwise provision a new cluster with an autogenerated name.
- `--dryrun` ‚Äî If True, do not actually run the job.
- `--detach-run`, `-d`, `-no-d` ‚Äî If True, as soon as a job is submitted, return from this call and do not stream execution logs.
- `--docker` ‚Äî (Deprecated) Local docker support is deprecated. To run locally, create a local Kubernetes cluster with ``sky local up``.
- `--name`, `-n` ‚Äî Task name. Overrides the "name" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--workdir` ‚Äî If specified, sync this dir to the remote working directory, where the task will be invoked. Overrides the "workdir" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, k8s/context-name, or ssh/node-pool-name. Examples: aws, aws/us-east-1, aws/us-east-1/us-east-1a, aws/\*/us-east-1a, k8s/my-contex...
- `--cloud` ‚Äî The cloud to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.cloud" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--num-nodes` ‚Äî Number of nodes to execute the task on. Overrides the "num_nodes" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--cpus` ‚Äî Number of vCPUs each instance must have (e.g., ``--cpus=4`` (exactly 4) or ``--cpus=4+`` (at least 4)). This is used to automatically select the instance type.
- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--env-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with environment variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--env-file`` conflict with values set by ``--env``, the ``--env`` value will be preferred.  Values fro...
- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--idle-minutes-to-autostop`, `-i` ‚Äî Automatically stop the cluster after this many minutes of idleness, i.e., no running or pending jobs in the cluster's job queue. Idleness gets reset depending on the ``--wait-for`` flag. Setting this ...
- `--wait-for` ‚Äî Determines the condition for resetting the idleness timer. This option works in conjunction with ``--idle-minutes-to-autostop``. Options:   1. ``jobs_and_ssh`` (default): Wait for in-progress jobs an...
- `--down` ‚Äî Autodown the cluster: tear down the cluster after all jobs finish (successfully or abnormally). If --idle-minutes-to-autostop is also set, the cluster will be torn down after the specified idle time. ...
- `--retry-until-up`, `-r` ‚Äî Whether to retry provisioning infinitely until the cluster is up, if we fail to launch the cluster on any possible region/cloud due to unavailability errors.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--no-setup` ‚Äî Skip setup phase when (re-)launching cluster.
- `--clone-disk-from`, `--clone` ‚Äî [Experimental] Clone disk from an existing cluster to launch a new one. This is useful when the new cluster needs to have the same data on the boot disk as an existing cluster.
- `--fast` ‚Äî [Experimental] If the cluster is already up and available, skip provisioning and setup steps.
- `--git-url` ‚Äî Git repository URL.
- `--git-ref` ‚Äî Git reference (branch, tag, or commit hash) to use.

### `sky exec`

Execute a task or command on an existing cluster.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTER` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to diagnose.
- `--cluster`, `-c` ‚Äî This is the same as the positional argument, just for consistency.
- `ENTRYPOINT` ‚Äî text
- `--detach-run`, `-d` ‚Äî If True, as soon as a job is submitted, return from this call and do not stream execution logs.
- `--name`, `-n` ‚Äî Task name. Overrides the "name" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--workdir` ‚Äî If specified, sync this dir to the remote working directory, where the task will be invoked. Overrides the "workdir" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, k8s/context-name, or ssh/node-pool-name. Examples: aws, aws/us-east-1, aws/us-east-1/us-east-1a, aws/\*/us-east-1a, k8s/my-contex...
- `--cloud` ‚Äî The cloud to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.cloud" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--num-nodes` ‚Äî Number of nodes to execute the task on. Overrides the "num_nodes" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--cpus` ‚Äî Number of vCPUs each instance must have (e.g., ``--cpus=4`` (exactly 4) or ``--cpus=4+`` (at least 4)). This is used to automatically select the instance type.
- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--env-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with environment variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--env-file`` conflict with values set by ``--env``, the ``--env`` value will be preferred.  Values fro...
- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--git-url` ‚Äî Git repository URL.
- `--git-ref` ‚Äî Git reference (branch, tag, or commit hash) to use.

### `sky status`

Show clusters.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `--refresh`, `-r` ‚Äî Query the latest cluster statuses from the cloud provider(s).
- `--ip` ‚Äî Get the IP address of the head node of a cluster. This option will override all other options. For Kubernetes clusters, the returned IP address is the internal IP of the head pod, and may not be acces...
- `--endpoints` ‚Äî Get all exposed endpoints and corresponding URLs for acluster. This option will override all other options.
- `--endpoint` ‚Äî Get the endpoint URL for the specified port number on the cluster. This option will override all other options.
- `--show-managed-jobs`, `--no-show-managed-jobs` ‚Äî Also show recent in-progress managed jobs, if any.
- `--show-services`, `--no-show-services` ‚Äî Also show sky serve services, if any.
- `--show-pools`, `--no-show-pools` ‚Äî Also show pools, if any.
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Show all clusters, including those not owned by the current user.
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

### `sky stop`

Stop cluster(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Stop all existing clusters.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Stop all existing clusters for all users.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--graceful` ‚Äî Wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads to complete before stopping/terminating. Will cancel current jobs first.
- `--graceful-timeout` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for `--graceful` flag. When not set, will wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads until they are finished.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky start`

Restart cluster(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Start all existing clusters.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--idle-minutes-to-autostop`, `-i` ‚Äî Automatically stop the cluster after this many minutes of idleness, i.e., no running or pending jobs in the cluster's job queue. Idleness gets reset depending on the ``--wait-for`` flag. Setting this ...
- `--wait-for` ‚Äî Determines the condition for resetting the idleness timer. This option works in conjunction with ``--idle-minutes-to-autostop``. Options:   1. ``jobs_and_ssh`` (default): Wait for in-progress jobs an...
- `--down` ‚Äî Autodown the cluster: tear down the cluster after specified minutes of idle time after all jobs finish (successfully or abnormally). Requires --idle-minutes-to-autostop to be set.
- `--retry-until-up`, `-r` ‚Äî Retry provisioning infinitely until the cluster is up, if we fail to start the cluster due to unavailability errors.
- `--force`, `-f` ‚Äî Force start the cluster even if it is already UP. Useful for upgrading the SkyPilot runtime on the cluster.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky down`

Tear down cluster(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Tear down all existing clusters.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Tear down all existing clusters for all users.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--purge`, `-p` ‚Äî (Advanced) Forcefully remove the cluster(s) from SkyPilot's cluster table, even if the actual cluster termination failed on the cloud. WARNING: This flag should only be set sparingly in certain manual...
- `--graceful` ‚Äî Wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads to complete before stopping/terminating. Will cancel current jobs first.
- `--graceful-timeout` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for `--graceful` flag. When not set, will wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads until they are finished.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky autostop`

Schedule an autostop or autodown for cluster(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Autostop all existing clusters.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Autostop all existing clusters for all users.
- `--idle-minutes`, `-i` ‚Äî Set the idle minutes before autostopping the cluster. See the doc above for detailed semantics.
- `--wait-for` ‚Äî Determines the condition for resetting the idleness timer. This option works in conjunction with ``--idle-minutes``. Options:   1. ``jobs_and_ssh`` (default): Wait for in-progress jobs and SSH connec...
- `--cancel` ‚Äî Cancel any currently active auto{stop,down} setting for the cluster. No-op if there is no active setting.
- `--down` ‚Äî Use autodown (tear down the cluster; non-restartable), instead of autostop (restartable).
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky cost-report`

Show estimated costs for launched clusters.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Show all cluster information.
- `--days` (default: `30`) ‚Äî Show clusters from the last N days. Default is 30 days. If set to 0, show all clusters.

## Job Queue Commands

### `sky queue`

Show the job queue for cluster(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Show all users' information in full.
- `--skip-finished`, `-s` ‚Äî Show only pending/running jobs' information.
- `CLUSTERS` ‚Äî text
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

### `sky logs`

Tail the log of a job.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--provision` ‚Äî Stream the cluster provisioning logs (provision.log).
- `--autostop` ‚Äî Stream the autostop hook logs from the cluster.
- `--worker`, `-w` ‚Äî The worker ID to stream the logs from. If not set, stream the logs of the head node.
- `--sync-down`, `-s` ‚Äî Sync down the logs of a job to the local machine. For a distributed job, a separate log file from each worker will be downloaded.
- `--status` ‚Äî If specified, do not show logs but exit with a status code for the job's status: 0 for succeeded, or 1 for all other statuses.
- `--follow`, `--no-follow` ‚Äî Follow the logs of a job. If --no-follow is specified, print the log so far and exit. [default: --follow]
- `--tail` (default: `0`) ‚Äî The number of lines to display from the end of the log file. Default is 0, which means print all lines.
- `CLUSTER` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to diagnose.
- `JOB_IDS` ‚Äî text

### `sky cancel`

Cancel job(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTER` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to diagnose.
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Cancel all jobs from current user on the specified cluster.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Cancel all jobs on the specified cluster for all users.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `JOBS` ‚Äî integer

## Infrastructure Commands

### `sky check`

Check which clouds are available to use.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `INFRA_LIST` ‚Äî text
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show the activated account for each cloud.
- `--workspace`, `-w` ‚Äî The workspace to check. If None, all workspaces will be checked.
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

## Dashboard

### `sky dashboard`

Opens the SkyPilot dashboard.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).

## GPU/Accelerator Commands

SkyPilot GPU/Accelerator CLI.

### `sky gpus label`

Label GPU nodes in a Kubernetes cluster for use with SkyPilot.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--context`, `-c` ‚Äî Kubernetes context to use. If not specified, uses the current context from the API server.
- `--cleanup` ‚Äî Only cleanup existing GPU labeler resources.
- `--async` ‚Äî Do not wait for GPU labeling to complete.

### `sky gpus list`

Show supported GPU/TPU/accelerators and their prices.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `ACCELERATOR_STR` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Show details of all GPU/TPU/accelerator offerings.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to query. Examples: "aws", "aws/us-east-1"
- `--cloud` ‚Äî Cloud provider to query.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If not specified, shows accelerators from all regions.
- `--all-regions` ‚Äî Show pricing and instance details for a specified accelerator across all regions and clouds.
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

## Managed Jobs Commands

Managed Jobs CLI (jobs with auto-recovery).

### `sky jobs cancel`

Cancel managed jobs.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--name`, `-n` ‚Äî Managed job name to cancel.
- `--pool`, `-p` ‚Äî Pool name to cancel.
- `JOB_IDS` ‚Äî integer
- `--graceful` ‚Äî Wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads to complete before stopping/terminating. Will cancel current jobs first.
- `--graceful-timeout` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for `--graceful` flag. When not set, will wait for MOUNT_CACHED uploads until they are finished.
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Cancel all managed jobs for the current user.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Cancel all managed jobs from all users.

### `sky jobs dashboard`

Opens a dashboard for managed jobs.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).

### `sky jobs launch`

Launch a managed job from a YAML or a command.

- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.

- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.

- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--cluster`, `-c` ‚Äî Alias for --name, the name of the managed job.
- `--job-recovery` ‚Äî Recovery strategy to use for managed jobs.
- `--detach-run`, `-d` ‚Äî If True, as soon as a job is submitted, return from this call and do not stream execution logs.
- `--pool`, `-p` ‚Äî (Experimental; optional) Pool to use for jobs submission.
- `--num-jobs` ‚Äî Number of jobs to submit.
- `--git-url` ‚Äî Git repository URL.
- `--git-ref` ‚Äî Git reference (branch, tag, or commit hash) to use.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.

### `sky jobs logs`

Tail or sync down the log of a managed job.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--name`, `-n` ‚Äî Managed job name.
- `--follow`, `--no-follow` ‚Äî Follow the logs of the job. [default: --follow] If --no-follow is specified, print the log so far and exit.
- `--controller` ‚Äî Show the controller logs of this job; useful for debugging launching/recoveries, etc.
- `--refresh`, `-r` ‚Äî Query the latest job logs, restarting the jobs controller if stopped.
- `--sync-down`, `-s` ‚Äî Download logs for all jobs shown in the queue.
- `JOB_ID` ‚Äî integer
- `TASK` ‚Äî text

### `sky jobs pool` (subgroup)

(Experimental) Pool management commands.

### `sky jobs pool apply`

Either apply a config to a pool for managed jobs submission or update the number of workers in the pool. One of POOL_YAML or --workers must be provided.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `POOL_YAML` ‚Äî text
- `--pool`, `-p` ‚Äî A pool name. Unique for each pool. If not provided, a unique name is autogenerated.
- `--mode` (default: `rolling`) ‚Äî Update mode. If "rolling", pool will be updated with rolling update. If "blue_green", pool will be updated with blue-green update. This option is only valid when the pool is already running.
- `--workers` ‚Äî Can be used to update the number of workers in the pool.
- `--workdir` ‚Äî If specified, sync this dir to the remote working directory, where the task will be invoked. Overrides the "workdir" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, k8s/context-name, or ssh/node-pool-name. Examples: aws, aws/us-east-1, aws/us-east-1/us-east-1a, aws/\*/us-east-1a, k8s/my-contex...
- `--cloud` ‚Äî The cloud to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.cloud" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--num-nodes` ‚Äî Number of nodes to execute the task on. Overrides the "num_nodes" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--cpus` ‚Äî Number of vCPUs each instance must have (e.g., ``--cpus=4`` (exactly 4) or ``--cpus=4+`` (at least 4)). This is used to automatically select the instance type.
- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--env-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with environment variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--env-file`` conflict with values set by ``--env``, the ``--env`` value will be preferred.  Values fro...
- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.

### `sky jobs pool down`

Delete pool(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `POOL_NAMES` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Delete all pools.
- `--purge`, `-p` ‚Äî Tear down pools in failed status.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky jobs pool logs`

Tail or sync down logs of a pool.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--follow`, `--no-follow` ‚Äî Follow the logs of the job. [default: --follow] If --no-follow is specified, print the log so far and exit.
- `--controller` ‚Äî Show the controller logs of this pool.
- `--sync-down`, `-s` ‚Äî Sync down logs to the local machine. Can be combined with --controller or worker ID to narrow scope.
- `--tail` ‚Äî The number of lines to display from the end of the log file. Default is None, which means print all lines.
- `POOL_NAME` ‚Äî text
- `WORKER_IDS` ‚Äî integer

### `sky jobs pool status`

Show statuses of pools.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `POOL_NAMES` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Show all workers.

### `sky jobs queue`

Show statuses of managed jobs.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `--limit`, `-l` (default: `50`) ‚Äî Number of jobs to show, default is 50, use "-a/--all" to show all jobs.
- `--refresh`, `-r` ‚Äî Query the latest statuses, restarting the jobs controller if stopped.
- `--skip-finished`, `-s` ‚Äî Show only pending/running jobs' information.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Show jobs from all users.
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Show all jobs.
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

## SkyServe Commands

SkyServe CLI (multi-region, multi-cloud serving).

### `sky serve down`

Teardown service(s).

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `SERVICE_NAMES` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Tear down all services.
- `--purge`, `-p` ‚Äî Tear down services in failed status.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--replica-id` ‚Äî Tear down a given replica
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky serve logs`

Tail or sync down logs of a service.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--follow`, `--no-follow` ‚Äî Follow the logs of the job. [default: --follow] If --no-follow is specified, print the log so far and exit.
- `--controller` ‚Äî Show the controller logs of this service.
- `--load-balancer` ‚Äî Show the load balancer logs of this service.
- `--sync-down`, `-s` ‚Äî Sync down logs to the local machine. Can be combined with --controller, --load-balancer, or a replica ID to narrow scope.
- `--tail` ‚Äî The number of lines to display from the end of the log file. Default is None, which means print all lines.
- `SERVICE_NAME` ‚Äî text
- `REPLICA_IDS` ‚Äî integer

### `sky serve status`

Show statuses of SkyServe services.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `--endpoint` ‚Äî Show service endpoint.
- `SERVICE_NAMES` ‚Äî text

### `sky serve up`

Launch a SkyServe service.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `SERVICE_YAML` ‚Äî text
- `--service-name`, `-n` ‚Äî A service name. Unique for each service. If not provided, a unique name is autogenerated.
- `--git-url` ‚Äî Git repository URL.
- `--git-ref` ‚Äî Git reference (branch, tag, or commit hash) to use.
- `--workdir` ‚Äî If specified, sync this dir to the remote working directory, where the task will be invoked. Overrides the "workdir" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, k8s/context-name, or ssh/node-pool-name. Examples: aws, aws/us-east-1, aws/us-east-1/us-east-1a, aws/\*/us-east-1a, k8s/my-contex...
- `--cloud` ‚Äî The cloud to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.cloud" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--num-nodes` ‚Äî Number of nodes to execute the task on. Overrides the "num_nodes" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--cpus` ‚Äî Number of vCPUs each instance must have (e.g., ``--cpus=4`` (exactly 4) or ``--cpus=4+`` (at least 4)). This is used to automatically select the instance type.
- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.

- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.

### `sky serve update`

Update a SkyServe service.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `SERVICE_NAME` ‚Äî text
- `SERVICE_YAML` ‚Äî text
- `--workdir` ‚Äî If specified, sync this dir to the remote working directory, where the task will be invoked. Overrides the "workdir" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, k8s/context-name, or ssh/node-pool-name. Examples: aws, aws/us-east-1, aws/us-east-1/us-east-1a, aws/\*/us-east-1a, k8s/my-contex...
- `--cloud` ‚Äî The cloud to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.cloud" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--region` ‚Äî The region to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.region" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--zone` ‚Äî The zone to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.zone" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--num-nodes` ‚Äî Number of nodes to execute the task on. Overrides the "num_nodes" config in the YAML if both are supplied.
- `--cpus` ‚Äî Number of vCPUs each instance must have (e.g., ``--cpus=4`` (exactly 4) or ``--cpus=4+`` (at least 4)). This is used to automatically select the instance type.
- `--memory` ‚Äî Amount of memory each instance must have in GB (e.g., ``--memory=16`` (exactly 16GB), ``--memory=16+`` (at least 16GB))
- `--disk-size` ‚Äî OS disk size in GBs.
- `--disk-tier` ‚Äî OS disk tier. Could be one of low, medium, high, ultra, best, none. If best is specified, use the best possible disk tier. If none is specified, enforce to use default value and override the option in...
- `--network-tier` ‚Äî Network tier. Could be one of standard, best. If best is specified, use the best network tier available on the specified instance. Default: standard
- `--use-spot`, `--no-use-spot` ‚Äî Whether to request spot instances. If specified, overrides the "resources.use_spot" config.
- `--image-id` ‚Äî Custom image id for launching the instances. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--env-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with environment variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--env-file`` conflict with values set by ``--env``, the ``--env`` value will be preferred.  Values fro...
- `--env` ‚Äî Environment variable to set on the remote node. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--env MY_ENV=1``: set ``$MY_ENV`` on the cluster to be 1.  2. ``--env MY_ENV2=$HOME``: set ``$MY_E...
- `--secret-file` ‚Äî Path to a dotenv file with secret variables to set on the remote node.  If any values from ``--secret-file`` conflict with values set by ``--secret``, the ``--secret`` value will be preferred.
- `--secret` ‚Äî Secret variable to set on the remote node. These variables will be redacted in logs and YAML outputs for security. It can be specified multiple times. Examples:   1. ``--secret API_KEY=secret123``: s...
- `--local-disk` ‚Äî Local (instance) storage requirement. Format: [mode:]size[+] or mode. Mode is "nvme" (default) or "ssd". Size is total GB across all disks. If only mode specified, size defaults to 100+. If only size ...
- `--gpus` ‚Äî Type and number of GPUs to use. Example values: "V100:8", "V100" (short for a count of 1), or "V100:0.5" (fractional counts are supported by the scheduling framework). If a new cluster is being launch...
- `--instance-type`, `-t` ‚Äî The instance type to use. If specified, overrides the "resources.instance_type" config. Passing "none" resets the config.
- `--ports` ‚Äî Ports to open on the cluster. If specified, overrides the "ports" config in the YAML.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--mode` (default: `rolling`) ‚Äî Update mode. If "rolling", SkyServe will update the service with rolling update. If "blue_green", SkyServe will update the service with blue-green update.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.

## Storage Commands

SkyPilot Storage CLI.

### `sky storage delete`

Delete storage objects.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `NAMES` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Delete all storage objects.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky storage ls`

List storage objects managed by SkyPilot.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.

## Volume Commands

SkyPilot Volumes CLI.

### `sky volumes apply`

Apply a volume.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `ENTRYPOINT` ‚Äî text
- `--name`, `-n` ‚Äî Volume name. Override the name defined in the YAML.
- `--infra` ‚Äî Infrastructure to use. Format: cloud, cloud/region, cloud/region/zone, or k8s/context-name.Examples: k8s, k8s/my-context, runpod/US/US-CA-2. Override the infra defined in the YAML.
- `--type` ‚Äî Volume type. Override the type defined in the YAML.
- `--size` ‚Äî Volume size. Override the size defined in the YAML.
- `--use-existing`, `--no-use-existing` ‚Äî Whether to use an existing volume. Override the use_existing defined in the YAML.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky volumes delete`

Delete volumes.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `NAMES` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Delete all volumes.
- `--purge`, `-p` ‚Äî Forcibly delete the volume from the volumes table even if the deletion API fails.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.
- `--async`, `--no-async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky volumes ls`

List volumes managed by SkyPilot.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show all information in full.
- `--refresh`, `-r` ‚Äî Refresh volume state from cloud APIs before listing. Without this flag, cached data is returned which is updated periodically by the background daemon.

## API Server Commands

SkyPilot API server commands.

### `sky api cancel`

Cancel a request running on SkyPilot API server.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `REQUEST_IDS` ‚Äî text
- `--all`, `-a` ‚Äî Cancel all your requests.
- `--all-users`, `-u` ‚Äî Cancel all requests from all users.
- `--yes`, `-y` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt.

### `sky api info`

Shows the SkyPilot API server URL.

**Options:**

- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.
- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).

### `sky api login`

Logs into a SkyPilot API server.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `--endpoint`, `-e` ‚Äî The SkyPilot API server endpoint.
- `--relogin` ‚Äî Force relogin with OAuth2 when enabled.
- `--service-account-token`, `--token`, `-t` ‚Äî Service account token for authentication (starts with ``sky_``).

### `sky api logout`

Logs out of the api server

### `sky api logs`

Stream the logs of a request running on SkyPilot API server.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `REQUEST_ID` ‚Äî text
- `--server-logs` ‚Äî Stream the server logs.
- `--log-path`, `-l` ‚Äî The path to the log file to stream.
- `--tail` ‚Äî Number of lines to show from the end of the logs. (default: None)
- `--follow`, `--no-follow` ‚Äî Follow the logs.

### `sky api start`

Starts the SkyPilot API server locally.

**Options:**

- `--deploy` ‚Äî Deploy the SkyPilot API server. When set to True, SkyPilot API server will use all resources on the host machine assuming the machine is dedicated to SkyPilot API server; host will also be set to 0.0....
- `--host` (default: `127.0.0.1`) ‚Äî The host to deploy the SkyPilot API server. To allow remote access, set this to 0.0.0.0
- `--foreground` ‚Äî Run the SkyPilot API server in the foreground and output its logs to stdout/stderr. Allowing external systems to manage the process lifecycle and collect logs directly. This is useful when the API ser...
- `--enable-basic-auth` ‚Äî Enable basic authentication in the SkyPilot API server.

### `sky api status`

List requests on SkyPilot API server.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `REQUEST_ID_PREFIXES` ‚Äî text
- `--all-status`, `-a` ‚Äî Show requests of all statuses, including finished ones (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELLED). By default, only active requests (PENDING, RUNNING) are shown.
- `--limit`, `-l` (default: `50`) ‚Äî Number of requests to show, default is 50, set to "none" or "all" to show all requests.
- `--cluster`, `-c` ‚Äî Filter request by cluster name.
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show more details.
- `--output`, `-o` (default: `table`) ‚Äî Output format. Choices: table, json. Default: table.

### `sky api stop`

Stops the SkyPilot API server locally.

## SSH Node Pool Commands

Commands for managing SSH Node Pools.

### `sky ssh down`

Clean up a cluster set up with 'sky ssh up'.

**Options:**

- `--infra` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to clean up in ~/.sky/ssh_node_pools.yaml. If not specified, all clusters in the file will be cleaned up.
- `--async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.

### `sky ssh up`

Set up a cluster using SSH targets from a file. If not specified, ~/.sky/ssh_node_pools.yaml will be used.

**Options:**

- `--infra` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to set up in ~/.sky/ssh_node_pools.yaml. If not specified, all clusters in the file will be set up.
- `--async` ‚Äî Run the command asynchronously.
- `--file`, `-f` ‚Äî The file containing the SSH targets.

## Other Commands

### `sky doctor`

Run accelerator health diagnostics on a cluster.

**Options:**

- `--config` ‚Äî Path to a config file or a single key-value pair. To add multiple key-value pairs add multiple flags (e.g. --config nested.key1=val1 --config nested.key2=val2).
- `CLUSTER` ‚Äî Name of the cluster to diagnose.
- `--verbose`, `-v` ‚Äî Show detailed output for each check, including raw command output.
